title: vitex triflora f. quinquefoliolata
intro: Vitex triflora f. quinquefoliolata, also known as Chasteberry, is a plant species in the Verbenaceae family, native to Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-anxiety effects. The health benefits of Vitex triflora f. quinquefoliolata include alleviating symptoms of PMS, menopausal hot flashes, and menstrual cramps, as well as supporting reproductive health. Herbal preparations of Vitex triflora f. quinquefoliolata include capsules, teas, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
